items.title: Railways announces unified licensing for container train operations
items.summary: Indian Railways has unified container train operator licenses for all routes. This reform aims to encourage greater private sector participation in containerized trade. Fertilizer freight tariffs are now rationalized and can be transported in containers. Individual fertilizer containers can be stored at rake points for flexible distribution.

items.title: RBI proposes easing rules for mutual funds, insurers to acquire higher stake in banks
items.summary: The RBI has proposed one-time approval for mutual funds, insurers and pension funds to increase bank shareholding up to 10% without repeated regulatory clearance. The move aims to simplify compliance for significant institutional investors, with public comments invited until August 4, 2026.

items.title: DP World to pilot atmospheric water generation technology across global operations after strategic investment in Maithri Aquatech
items.summary: Maithri Aquatech develops atmospheric water generation systems that produce potable water from atmospheric humidity.

items.title: Rs 1,839 crore Biocon block deal: ICICI Pru MF biggest buyer along with Citi and Goldman Sachs
items.summary: Mylan sold a ₹1,839 crore stake in Biocon through a block deal, attracting strong institutional demand. ICICI Prudential Mutual Fund emerged as the largest buyer, alongside other mutual funds, insurers and foreign investors, highlighting continued confidence in the pharmaceutical company.

items.title: IBM stock craters 23% after issuing second-quarter earnings warning
items.summary: CEO blamed the shortfall on weakness in the software and infrastructure business because clients shifted money toward hardware purchases.

items.title: Thomson Reuters to sell 51% stake in global print business to KKR for $500 million
items.summary: Thomson Reuters plans to divest its majority stake in the Global Print business to KKR, enabling a renewed focus on core operations. Set to finalize in the fourth quarter of 2026, this arrangement allows KKR exclusive distribution rights for both print and digital formats, while Thomson Reuters will maintain intellectual property and editorial oversight of the content.

items.title: India’s Rampur to represent Asia in global whisky climate study
items.summary: In an exciting development, Rampur Distillery will participate in a groundbreaking international climate study that focuses on whisky maturation. This six-month examination will involve eight unique single malts from different climates, utilizing both chemical testing and sensory analysis to explore how climate influences flavor. The results promise to provide valuable insights into the maturation process, further shining a light on the esteemed Indian single malt industry.

items.title: US inflation rate eases to 3.5% as gasoline prices fall
items.summary: While the US inflation rate fell in June, concerns remain over price rises increasing again due to the renewed conflict in the Middle East.

items.title: US stocks today: S&P 500 and Nasdaq open higher as CPI, bank earnings take focus
items.summary: US markets opened mixed as softer inflation data boosted expectations of a less aggressive Federal Reserve. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq gained, supported by rate cut hopes, while the Dow declined. Investors also tracked major bank earnings, balancing optimism on inflation with caution around corporate performance.

items.title: India's Purple Economy could add $150 billion to growth
items.summary: India's Purple Economy offers a USD 150 billion market opportunity. This framework recognizes persons with disabilities as economic contributors. Inclusion drives market creation and economic growth for the nation. Businesses designing for inclusion will build stronger markets and talent.

items.title: SEBI’s new conflict code shifts from disclosure to prevention
items.summary: Experts say new ethics framework strengthens governance through recusal, disclosures and oversight, while seeking greater clarity on subjective conflict provisions

items.summary: Treasury yields fell Tuesday after a cooler-than-expected inflation report.

items.summary: The non-institutional investors’ portion was subscribed 1.39 times, while the retail investors’ category saw subscription of 0.62 times

items.title: Keeping watch on West Asia developments, will support exporters if needed: Official
items.summary: With a vigilant eye on West Asia's evolving situation, India emphasizes its support for exporters amidst rising challenges. An Inter-Ministerial Group is actively monitoring the trade and energy implications resulting from the ongoing crisis. While exports to the UAE have thrived, Saudi Arabia has witnessed a drop. Notably, Indian exporters are turning to Omani ports as strategic hubs for regional trade activities.

items.title: Diamond giant De Beers halts work at flagship South African mine as demand plummets
items.summary: Production will stop for two years at the mine which employs more than 4,000 people.

items.title: Sebi exempts Juhi Chawla-owned Mehta Family Trust from Saurashtra Cement open offer
items.summary: The Securities and Exchange Board of India (Sebi) has granted the Mehta Family Trust relief from the open offer requirement linked to their indirect acquisition of shares in Saurashtra Cement Ltd. This restructuring aims to facilitate succession planning within the family and has been determined as non-commercial, ensuring that the control of the company remains unchanged. The one-year exemption will come with specific reporting obligations.

items.title: Adani ducks headwinds to record a huge jump in realty fortune; at cusp of being richest in biz: Report
items.summary: Last year marked a significant financial upturn for Gautam Adani's family, as their real estate holdings witnessed a remarkable increase, valuing Adani Properties at Rs 90,400 crore, up by Rs 38,000 crore. This contrast sharply with the broader real estate market, where the BSE Realty Index plummeted by 20 percent, impacting many companies negatively. DLF remains at the forefront, while Adani now ranks fourth among the richest realty tycoons.

items.title: JPMorgan posts record profit on big gains from dealmaking, stock trading
items.summary: In a remarkable feat, JPMorgan Chase announced its record profits for the second quarter, driven by impressive growth in investment banking and trading revenues. This quarter saw investment banking fees hitting their highest levels since 2021, and market revenues soared by thirty-five percent due to fluctuating trading conditions. Despite this success, share prices dipped after the bank raised expense forecasts for 2026, though net interest income continued to rise significantly.

items.title: United Airlines' new upsell: Keeping other travelers out of the middle seat

items.summary: Trump said he will make a "Speech to the Nation on Thursday evening, at 9 P.M. Eastern," without providing further details.

items.title: India, UK engaged in discussions on carbon tax issue: Commerce Secretary
items.summary: India and the United Kingdom are discussing Britain's carbon tax regulation. The UK plans to implement its Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism starting in 2027. This new carbon tax could impact Indian exports worth USD 775 million. Discussions on this carbon tax were part of free trade agreement negotiations. India's steel exports to the UK have secured market access through the pact.

items.title: IBM shares plunge 26% after Q2 earnings forecast falls short of estimates
items.summary: IBM shares plunged 26% to $228 after Q2 sales fell below estimates, with revenue at $17.2 billion. CEO Arvind Krishna noted customers are limiting spending. The stock faces its largest intraday decline since the 1980s. Other software firms also experienced losses amid these results.

items.title: Brussels to propose easing banks’ capital requirements
items.summary: Proposal follows similar moves by US and UK as lenders push for loosening of regulation

items.title: Infosys, Wipro ADRs crash up to 7% as IBM warning sends ripples across IT sector
items.summary: Infosys and Wipro ADRs fell after IBM forecast weaker-than-expected quarterly revenue, signalling enterprise spending is shifting from software to AI infrastructure. The warning heightened concerns over discretionary technology budgets, deal delays and growth prospects for Indian IT companies.

items.title: IOC reassures customers on fuel quality after social media posts
items.summary: Indian Oil Corporation conducted nearly ten thousand inspections and over eight thousand fuel quality tests. The company seeks to reassure customers amid social media concerns about fuel quality. Strict action is taken against dealers found breaching contractual obligations and quality standards. Special inspection teams were deployed to independently verify compliance at retail outlets. Customers are urged to report concerns directly to the company for investigation.

items.title: Bank of America Q2 net income rises on higher trading activities
items.summary: Bank of America reported a boost in its second-quarter net income, largely driven by a significant uptick in its trading activities. This surge in trading revenue helped offset other areas, leading to a stronger overall financial performance for the banking giant during the period.

items.title: Why reaching the right audience matters more than reaching everyone
items.summary: Reach alone no longer defines marketing success; relevance does. This piece argues that engaging decision-makers matters more than mass impressions, positioning The Economic Times' influential readership as the ideal platform for meaningful business impact. Partner With Us to reach India's decision-makers.

items.title: Wells Fargo tops profit estimates on interest income boost, trading boom
items.summary: Wells Fargo reported a significant profit increase in the second quarter. Strong trading desks and robust loan growth fueled this positive financial performance. Net income rose seventeen percent, exceeding analyst expectations for the period. The bank's investment banking fees also saw a substantial jump. Headcount reduction continued as part of a long-term growth strategy.

items.title: Global shipping industry sounds the alarm over Trump’s Hormuz toll plan
items.summary: For shipping giant Hapag-Lloyd, it is "fundamentally wrong" to charge tolls for passage through international waters, regardless of the country in charge.

items.title: SBI Funds Management IPO subscribed 0.71x on Day 1; NII portion leads demand, GMP signals 16% listing premium
items.summary: SBI Funds Management's IPO saw strong investor interest on Day 1, with overall subscription at 0.71 times. Retail and NII segments showed positive engagement, while QIB interest was lower. The IPO, valued at ₹9,812.91 crore, is an Offer for Sale with SBI and Amundi as main shareholders.

items.title: IKEA India plans to double investment to over Rs 21,000 cr by 2030; targets Rs 8,000 cr turnover
items.summary: Swedish retailer IKEA plans to invest over Rs 21,000 crore in India by 2030. This significant investment will accelerate store expansion and strengthen local sourcing operations. The company aims to quadruple its turnover to Rs 8,000 crore by the end of the decade. IKEA also intends to double its workforce and sourcing volumes from India. This expansion underscores IKEA's long-term confidence in the Indian market.

items.title: Warren Buffett excludes Gates Foundation from his annual donations of Berkshire stock
items.summary: Warren Buffett omitted the Gates Foundation from his annual charitable stock gifts.

items.title: Vedanta AGM: Anil Agarwal maps aggressive expansion after demerger, targets scale-up across metals, oil and power
items.summary: Vedanta plans major expansion across its demerged businesses, targeting higher production in metals, oil, gas, steel and power. Chairman Anil Agarwal highlighted record FY2026 performance, AI-led efficiency, critical mineral exploration and ambitious capacity additions to drive long-term growth.

items.title: Indo-MIM said to cut IPO size to $420 million in July launch
items.summary: Precision engineering firm Indo-MIM is set to launch its IPO later this month, targeting around $420 million after trimming the issue size from an earlier $700 million plan. The offering, delayed due to Middle East tensions, comes amid improving market sentiment and renewed foreign investor interest in Indian equities.

items.title: Warsh promises inflation will be a 'thing of the past,' cites benefits of AI investment boom
items.summary: Warsh pledged Tuesday to "get monetary policy right" and defeat the inflation that has bedeviled the central bank for the past five years.

items.title: Quote of the day by Jean Marie Eveillard: "Risk to us goes back to not paying attention to how one does in the short term"
items.summary: Value investor Jean-Marie Eveillard emphasizes long-term focus over short-term market volatility. He argues that focusing on daily price movements leads to emotional and wealth-eroding decisions. Patience and business quality are key competitive advantages for disciplined investors. History shows markets move in cycles, rewarding those who stay the course. Greatest risk lies in letting short-term noise dictate long-term investment choices.

items.title: Market wrap: Top gainers and losers on Nifty and Sensex today
items.summary: Indian benchmark indices ended sharply lower on Tuesday as escalating Middle East tensions and surging crude oil prices triggered broad-based selling. Sensex fell 561 points and Nifty slipped below 24,100, with auto, IT, PSU banks and realty leading losses, while pharma stocks outperformed amid a flight to defensive sectors.

items.title: India, China trade rises to USD 91.72 bn in first 6-month, trade deficit widens to USD 67.1 bn
items.summary: China's exports to India grew significantly in early 2026. Indian exports to China also saw substantial percentage increases during this period. Bilateral trade between the two nations reached over ninety-one billion dollars. India's trade deficit with China widened to sixty-seven billion dollars. India seeks greater access for its pharmaceuticals and IT products to China.

items.title: UK wasted £10bn on PPE that left NHS staff poorly protected, Covid inquiry finds
items.summary: Healthcare staff were unable to properly protect themselves, or those in their care, from dangerous infections, says the latest inquiry report.

items.title: NBCC bags Rs 501.45-cr contracts, biggest in Rajasthan, to builds labs in 922 govt schools
items.summary: State-owned NBCC has secured four new contracts valued at Rs 501.45 crore. The company will construct 2,256 science laboratories for Rajasthan schools on a turnkey basis. NBCC also received a Rs 60.61-crore engineering contract from Bharat Electronics Limited. Power Finance Corporation awarded two CSR initiative contracts to NBCC for facilitation centres. These projects include construction work in Mizoram and Goa for educational purposes.

items.title: Zydex eyes Rs 300 cr revenue from paints business in 2 years
items.summary: Zydex Group is setting ambitious goals, targeting a tripling of revenue in their paints division, aiming for an impressive Rs 300 crore within the next two years. This surge is poised to be propelled by the rise of silicate mineral paints in the Indian market. To support this initiative, Zydex is investing Rs 50 crore into their Vadodara facility, while also collaborating with M3M India to enhance paint distribution.

items.title: US stock market today: S&P, Dow futures steady as investors await inflation data; crude at one-month high
items.summary: US stock futures traded mixed as energy prices surged, raising expectations of a Federal Reserve rate hike. Amid heightened Middle East tensions following US strikes on Iran and a cargo fee, investors await key earnings reports and inflation data.

items.title: IBM stock tumbles 23% in premarket trading as preliminary quarterly results fall below expectations
items.summary: IBM shares tumbled sharply in premarket trade after the company flagged weaker-than-expected second-quarter results, missing both profit and revenue estimates. Management attributed the shortfall to delayed deal closures and shifting client spending toward hardware ahead of price hikes, while cybersecurity concerns further weighed on performance during the quarter.

items.title: LML Realty to invest Rs 250 cr to develop 45-acre industrial park in Haryana
items.summary: LML Realty is set to launch a state-of-the-art industrial park in Haryana, with a substantial investment of Rs 250 crore aimed at supporting MSMEs. This initiative provides a range of real estate solutions tailored for manufacturers and has received approval under the Haryana government's PADMA scheme. In an ambitious effort, LML Realty has plans to establish 100 industrial parks throughout India, promoting growth and innovation.

items.title: Oil spike jolts Indian bonds, 10-year yield hits three-week high
items.summary: On Tuesday, Indian government bonds faced a notable downturn, with the benchmark 10-year yield climbing to a three-week peak as global market anxiety intensified. The recent spike in oil prices following tensions in the U.S.-Iran relationship has shaken investor confidence, resulting in a weaker rupee and declining stock values in Mumbai. Attention now turns to an impending decision on including Indian bonds in the global index.

items.title: India Inc's overseas fundraise picks up pace in May to $4.7 bn: RBI data
items.summary: Indian companies' external commercial borrowings proposals saw a significant jump in May. Total filings reached USD 4.74 billion, a notable increase from April's figures. Prominent firms like Indian Railway Finance Corporation and NTPC filed substantial borrowing intentions. Kisetsu Saison Finance also submitted multiple proposals for on-lending purposes. Filings for rupee-denominated bonds also occurred, with Sahrudaya Health-Care filing proposals.

items.title: TikTok policy chief defends safety measures amid EU push to limit children's social media access
items.summary: TikTok's policy chief defended the platform's safety measures as the European Union pushes ahead with restricting children's access to social media.

items.title: What is the Index of Services Production and which sectors does it cover?
items.summary: India's Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation has launched a new monthly services production index. This experimental index, with 2024-25 as its base year, covers 19 formal services sub-sectors. The first release for April 2026 shows broad-based growth across most services categories. This new indicator aims to complement the industrial production index for better economic monitoring. The trial series will help assess data quality and methodology before finalization.

items.title: Truecaller CEO: Extending TRAI's jurisdiction encroaches on MeitY's domain
items.summary: Truecaller's CEO Rishit Jhunjhunwala views TRAI's expanded jurisdiction as an encroachment on the IT Ministry's regulatory authority. He argues that compelling IT intermediaries to dictate user information infringes upon existing frameworks. This regulatory dispute centers on the labelling of calls from specific number series. Truecaller maintains that spammers exploit these numbers, violating TRAI's directives. The company advocates for collaborative solutions to enhance consumer protection and transparency.

items.title: How much should you give to the year-end teacher collection?
items.summary: Collecting money for a joint present might overcome the competitive gift-giving, but it can still cause friction.

items.title: 10 Essential Skills Students Should Learn Before Their First Job
items.summary: A degree may help students qualify for their first job, but succeeding in the real world requires skills that are rarely taught in classrooms. From using AI effectively and communicating ideas with confidence to understanding money and thinking like an entrepreneur, here are 10 essential skills every student should start building before entering the workforce.

items.title: Net direct tax collections rise 16% so far in FY27
items.summary: The higher collections strengthen the Centre's revenue position in the early months of FY27, giving it greater fiscal room to pursue its budgeted spending plans.

items.title: India, Maldives conclude first round of FTA talks; make progress across key negotiating areas
items.summary: India and Maldives concluded their first Free Trade Agreement negotiations. Both nations made substantial progress across eight technical sessions held virtually. Ministers reviewed the talks and reaffirmed commitment to expedite the agreement. Bilateral trade between India and Maldives has seen significant growth. The proposed FTA seeks to enhance market access and economic cooperation.

items.title: Crude surge, geopolitical jitters pull D-Street lower; Rupee hits month’s low
items.summary: The Nifty 50 closed at 24,052, down 158 points or 0.66%, while the BSE Sensex settled at 77,054, shedding 561 points or 0.72%

items.summary: Volkswagen is considering an additional 50,000 job cuts on top of the existing 50,000 already planned, as part of a major restructuring amid rising pressures from US tariffs, competition in China, and profitability concerns in electric vehicles.

items.title: India launches new services output index; 14 of 19 sectors post double-digit growth in April
items.summary: India's new Index of Services Production reveals strong growth in April 2026. Fourteen of nineteen formal services sub-sectors achieved double-digit expansion. Wholesale trade, retail, and accommodation led this impressive economic performance. Air and railway transport experienced declines, while other sectors showed positive annual growth. This index provides crucial monthly data on India's formal services sector.

items.summary: Moody's Ratings has confirmed the ratings for both State Bank of India and HDFC Bank, attributing a stable outlook to their robust asset quality. SBI excels with its diverse lending practices and stable net interest margins, while HDFC Bank is bolstered by a strong retail presence that ensures a low-cost deposit base. The agency forecasts that asset quality for both banks will remain stable moving forward.

items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Stock Price Highlights: Stock market benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty tumbled on Tuesday as a sharp surge in crude oil prices due to the renewed flare-up in West Asia dented investors' sentiment.

items.title: India poised to become one of world's top premium alcobev markets: Diageo India CEO
items.summary: New Delhi is set to become a major premium alcoholic beverage market in the next decade. United Spirits Ltd, also known as Diageo India, plans to lead this significant market evolution. The company sees India's economic expansion creating enduring opportunities for agile and ambitious businesses. Consumers are increasingly trading up to premium brands and engaging with them in new ways.

items.title: Retail Inflation up to 4.4% on costlier food, transport
items.summary: India's retail inflation reached 4.4% in June, surpassing the central bank's target. Higher food and transport costs drove this increase, marking the highest print under the new CPI series. Food inflation accelerated to 5.3%, crossing the five percent mark for the first time. Core inflation moderated to 3.9%, partly due to falling gold and silver prices. Economists anticipate the Reserve Bank of India will maintain its current policy rate.

items.title: India-UK CETA duty benefits to require CBIC digital authentication
items.summary: India will implement digital authentication for UK exporter origin declarations starting July 15. This new process requires customs to issue a Unique Reference Number before duty benefits are granted. UK exporters must email declarations to designated Indian customs email addresses for verification. Indian customs will authenticate data shared by UK authorities for this purpose. This system aims to prevent fraudulent claims and ensure compliance with trade agreement rules.

Both nations aim to boost investments and collaborations in education and talent development.
